content: Ari flagged James's GitHub profile during backend hiring push. Strong systems design work, particularly around distributed caching. Pulled him into initial screening list.
summary: Ari identified James as backend candidate via GitHub

content: Scheduled phone screen with James for Feb 18. Ari prepped talking points on our query optimization challenges. James apparently worked at scale before.
summary: James phone screen scheduled for Feb 18

content: Phone screen with James went 45 min, ran long. Asked tough questions about our embedding pipeline bottleneck. He identified two potential refactors immediately. Ari was impressed.
summary: James phone screen: strong on pipeline architecture

content: James has production experience scaling systems to 100M+ events/day. Previously at infrastructure team at mid-stage fintech. Comfortable with Postgres, Redis, event streaming. No Python, but picks up languages fast.
summary: James background: fintech infra, event streaming, scale

content: Coordinated onsite for James on Mar 2. Scheduled with Ari (systems design deep dive), Devon (architecture review), Jenny (casual lunch, culture fit), Lina (product context).
summary: James onsite scheduled Mar 2, four-person panel

content: Anya asked about James's comp expectations in prep. He said $200-230k base range, open on equity if upside clear. Flagged he's currently in backfill period, loose timeline.
summary: James comp expectations: $200-230k base, flexible equity

content: Mar 2 onsite. James pushed back on Devon's proposed SQL denormalization schema for agent state snapshots—too brittle, suggested event sourcing pattern instead. Team respected the critique. Not a pushover.
summary: James onsite: questioned SQL design, advocated for events

content: Debrief with Ari, Devon, Lina after James's onsite. All three thumbs up. Ari: 'He'll push back, which we need.' Devon: 'Knows what he doesn't know.' Lina liked his product intuition.
summary: Post-onsite debrief: unanimous positive signals

content: Drafted offer for James: $215k base, 0.4% equity, 4-year vest. Sent to Anya for approval before extending. Positioned senior IC track, not management.
summary: James offer: $215k base, 0.4% equity, 4yr vest

content: Decision: 0.4% equity for James reflects senior backend level and market rate for Series B SaaS. 0.3% would've felt tight given his seniority and what we've offered recent hires.
summary: 0.4% equity justified by seniority and market

content: Extended offer to James via email. Gave him until Mar 10 EOD to decide. Emphasized timeline to onboard before Q2 roadmap crunch. No pressure, but signal the urgency.
summary: Offer extended to James, decision deadline Mar 10

content: James asked for 24hr extension on offer decision. Mentioned competing offer from Stripe, but told them he wanted to think through Clude's product vision fit. Granted extension to Mar 11.
summary: James requested extension due to competing Stripe offer

content: James accepted offer Mar 12, 2pm. Asked to start Apr 1 for short notice period at current employer. Requested onboarding focus on system architecture, not intro docs. Smart self-direction.
summary: James accepted offer, starts Apr 1, wants arch deep-dive

content: Notified Ari, Devon, Lina that James accepted. Ari immediately blocked calendar for Apr 1-3 systems design sessions. Devon volunteered to prep codebase walkthrough.
summary: Team notified of James acceptance, onboarding blocked

content: When assessing senior backend candidates: (1) check GitHub for systems work first, (2) phone screen on specific infrastructure problem, (3) onsite should include architecture debate, not just trivia, (4) cultural signal is intellectual honesty, pushback = good.
summary: Senior backend hiring process crystallized from James search

content: James's pushback on SQL schema in onsite—event sourcing counter-proposal—is now reference point for 'the kind of thinking we want.' Mentioned it to Priya for future senior IC interviews.
summary: James's arch critique sets bar for senior IC rigor

content: Anya asked about James's start-date payroll. Confirmed Apr 1 is firm. Priya to set up equipment order, Slack access, GitHub org. Tom will do basic infra onboarding.
summary: Payroll and admin setup confirmed for James Apr 1 start

content: James hiring took 32 days start-to-accept. Faster than usual, helped by Ari's sourcing and team's speed to consensus. Realized I don't loop in enough on competitive offers—should ask candidates earlier, adjust timeline if needed.
summary: James hire was fast; need better competitive offer intel

content: Slack note to Sebastien: James confirmed, starts Apr 1, $215k base, 0.4%, strong fit on systems design. Mentioned he flagged SQL schema risk in onsite—good signal he'll improve codebase thinking.
summary: Updated Sebastien on James acceptance and onsite impression

content: Moved James into 'Active Hires' spreadsheet. Flagged for Q1 board update: senior backend slot filled, now open to evaluate frontend/data roles for Q2 hiring.
summary: James tracked in hiring pipeline, frontend next

content: Stripe competing offer for James means market is tight for senior backend talent. If we want to close faster next time, need offers within 24-48hrs of interview loop, or risk losing to bigger players.
summary: Stripe competition signals need for faster offer turnaround

content: Added James to Apr all-hands slide deck under 'New Team Members.' Ari prepping brief tech intro (no spoilers on system work yet). First day landing pad: systems docs, codebase tour, then deep dives.
summary: James added to all-hands, Apr 1 onboarding deck underway
